name: empty_map_nonempty_vec
code:
proof fn phi_1_empty_map_nonempty_vec(m: CKeyHashMap)
    requires
        m@.dom() === Set::<AbstractKey>::empty(),
    ensures
        m.spec_to_vec()@.len() > 0,
{
}
reason: An empty map should never produce a non-empty vector of key-value pairs

name: key_not_in_domain
code:
proof fn phi_2_key_not_in_domain(m: CKeyHashMap, i: int)
    requires
        0 <= i < m.spec_to_vec()@.len(),
    ensures
        !m@.contains_key(m.spec_to_vec()@[i].k),
{
}
reason: Every key in the to_vec result should be present in the map's domain

name: value_mismatch
code:
proof fn phi_3_value_mismatch(m: CKeyHashMap, i: int)
    requires
        0 <= i < m.spec_to_vec()@.len(),
        m@.contains_key(m.spec_to_vec()@[i].k),
    ensures
        m@[m.spec_to_vec()@[i].k] !== m.spec_to_vec()@[i].v@,
{
}
reason: The value for a key in to_vec should match the corresponding value in the map

name: duplicate_keys
code:
proof fn phi_4_duplicate_keys(m: CKeyHashMap, i: int, j: int)
    requires
        0 <= i < m.spec_to_vec()@.len(),
        0 <= j < m.spec_to_vec()@.len(),
        i != j,
    ensures
        m.spec_to_vec()@[i].k == m.spec_to_vec()@[j].k,
{
}
reason: The to_vec result should not contain duplicate keys since it represents a map

name: nonempty_map_empty_vec
code:
proof fn phi_5_nonempty_map_empty_vec(m: CKeyHashMap, k: AbstractKey)
    requires
        m@.contains_key(k),
    ensures
        m.spec_to_vec()@.len() == 0,
{
}
reason: A map with at least one key should never produce an empty vector representation
